reason: Both cover the core Goodhart/mean-skew/reclassification/frequency-vs-duration points and recommend similar replacement metrics (SLO/error-budget, incident frequency, pages per engineer, recurrence, percentiles). Answer A includes a redundant "Sketch" section that duplicates the subsequent prose almost verbatim, adding length without value for a busy reader. Answer B is more concise, adds a valid extra mechanism (survivorship bias — chronic issues/near-misses never becoming counted incidents), and closes with a concrete, testable diagnostic (plot incident count vs. total impact-minutes vs. MTTR) that directly operationalizes "how both can be true." This makes B marginally more useful and better organized despite both being materially correct.
